name: Elca
description_short: ELCA Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured credit union offering checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and home financing with competitive rates and no-fee accounts.
description_long:
ELCA Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered, member-owned credit union insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA). The organization serves a broad membership base with particular emphasis on serving ELCA (Evangelical Lutheran Church in America) communities and ministry leaders, though membership extends beyond religious affiliation. The credit union has been established as a trusted financial institution providing banking services and financial products to individuals and organizations within its field of membership.

ELCA FCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including no-fee checking and savings accounts, personal loans up to $25,000, auto loans starting at 3.49%, home loans with personalized service, credit cards with rates starting at 14.99% APR, and personal lines of credit up to $25,000. They provide youth accounts for ages 13-17 to build financial literacy, credit-building options for members strengthening their credit profiles, and specialized workplace banking programs for ministry organizations. Members also have access to 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide through their MasterCard debit card and mobile banking with remote deposit capture.

What distinguishes ELCA FCU is its dual focus on mainstream consumer lending combined with specialized ministry solutions. The Resourceful Servants program, developed jointly with the Mission Investment Fund, specifically addresses financial security for ELCA rostered leaders, seminarians, and congregations. The credit union emphasizes financial literacy education, offering free tools like SavvyMoney for credit monitoring and hosting Financial Literacy Month awareness programs. Their competitive auto loan rates (averaging $1,500+ in savings for refinances) and no-fee checking accounts (no minimum balance, free bill pay, free overdraft protection) are notable value propositions.

ELCA FCU is well-suited for members seeking community-oriented banking with competitive rates and no monthly fees, particularly those with ELCA affiliations or ministry involvement. The organization's strengths lie in auto lending, youth financial education, and specialized ministry financial programs. However, as a credit union rather than a bank, membership eligibility may be restricted based on employer, association, or geographic criteria, though their field of membership appears broad. Their rates are competitive but not necessarily industry-leading across all products.
services: No-fee checking accounts with free bill pay and overdraft protection
Youth checking and savings accounts for ages 13-17
Auto loans starting at 3.49% APR for up to 36 months
Personal loans up to $25,000 starting at 7.99% APR
Personal lines of credit up to $25,000 starting at 12.49% APR
Home loans with competitive rates for purchase, refinance, and home equity lines of credit
Credit card (World Mastercard) with 14.99% APR, no annual fee, no transaction fees
Debit card with access to 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide
Mobile banking app with remote deposit capture and ATM locator
Workplace banking program for ministry organizations and employers
Credit building options and financial literacy tools including SavvyMoney
pros: No monthly maintenance fees or minimum balance requirements on checking accounts
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 3.49% for up to 36 months with average savings of $1,500+ on refinances
Access to 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide through MasterCard debit card
Free mobile app with remote deposit capture and ATM locator
Specialized Resourceful Servants program for ELCA ministry leaders and congregations addressing financial wellness
Low-rate credit card with no annual fee (14.99% APR) and no transaction fees on purchases, balance transfers, or cash advances
Free financial tools including SavvyMoney for credit monitoring and score tracking
cons: Membership eligibility may be restricted based on field of membership criteria (specific to ELCA communities and affiliations)
Personal loan rates starting at 7.99% are competitive but not the lowest available in the market
Personal line of credit starting rate of 12.49% APR is relatively high compared to some alternative lenders
Limited information provided on website regarding savings account rates, CD rates, or mortgage-specific rates for comparison
As a credit union, fewer physical branch locations compared to major national banks
best_for: ELCA members and those with evangelical Lutheran church affiliations seeking community-focused banking
Borrowers looking to refinance auto loans and seeking rates below market average with no-fee checking
Ministry leaders, seminarians, and congregations needing specialized financial wellness programs and support
Teenagers and young adults aged 13-17 wanting to build foundational financial literacy with parental oversight
diagnosis: ELCA Federal Credit Union is best for ELCA members, ministry leaders, and individuals seeking community-oriented banking with competitive rates, no monthly fees, and strong auto lending options. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility depends on field of membership criteria, typically linked to ELCA affiliations or geographic/employment qualifications, so prospective members should verify their eligibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Elca — Credit Union in Chicago, IL | CreditDoc
meta_description: Elca — NCUA-insured credit union in Chicago, IL. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
